title: The Vision
author: Carl Sandburg
action: Articulate the vision before attempting the architecture.

title: The Divergence
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “If a man does not keep pace with his companions, perhaps it is because he hears a different drummer.”
action: Accept the isolation that comes with non-standard objectives.

title: The Immersion
author: Willa Cather
content: “That is happiness; to be dissolved into something complete and great.”
action: Surrender your ego to the demands of the craft.

title: The Obligation
author: Robert Frost
content: “The woods are lovely, dark and deep, But I have promises to keep, And miles to go before I sleep.”
action: Prioritize your commitments over the temptation of retreat.

title: The Impact
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
content: “To leave the world a bit better, whether by a healthy child, a garden patch, or a redeemed social condition.”
action: Measure the finality of your work by its tangible impact.
